paragraph: The single-family market in Cresskill is moving at a steady clip, with 44 homes sold over the last eight months at a median of $1,130,250. Sellers are pricing fairly close to list and homes are selling in the mid-range of days on market, which tells you this isn't a frenzy but it isn't soft either. About 39% of homes are selling above asking, which is moderate enough that a well-priced property will draw genuine competition without every listing turning into a bidding war. The range is wide, from $460,000 to over $4.2 million, so Cresskill isn't a single-price-point town. Condos and townhouses, by contrast, are a smaller slice with 11 sales at a median of $895,000. They're moving at the same pace as single-families in terms of days on market and with a similar percentage over asking, so both tiers are functioning competitively. If you're deciding between a single-family home and a condo in Cresskill, the market heat isn't the distinguishing factor. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family or the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo.
